content: Scout Report: wshobson/agents (28,870 stars, MIT, Python). 73-plugin marketplace for Claude Code orchestration. 112 agent definitions, 79 commands, 146 skills. Key patterns: (1) State-persistent multi-phase workflow orchestration via file-based intermediate state, (2) Three-tier progressive disclosure for skills, (3) Explicit model-tier assignment with inherit option, (4) Use-when activation triggers in agent descriptions, (5) File ownership invariant for parallel agents, (6) ACH methodology for parallel debugging. Anti-patterns: agent definition duplication across plugins, aspirational documentation presented as executable (improve-agent.md), no automated validation, version skew in docs. Dispatched architecture-consultant, docs-knowledge, qa-specialist, independent-perspective for co-review.

Three applicable patterns: (1) HIGH - State-persistent workflow orchestration via .full-review/*.md file pattern for multi-phase commands. Our /review, /deliberate, /analyze-project lack session resumption. (2) MEDIUM - inherit model tier absent from our framework. Allows cost-sensitive users to defer model selection. Low adoption cost. (3) LOW - File ownership invariant for parallel agents inapplicable now (our subagents are read-only reviewers). Plugin marketplace architecture not applicable at our scale. Conductor track management solves different problem domain.
